elements.name: Title
texts: "The silver strands of the individual sometimes show"

elements.name: Description
texts: The poetry of Patrick Kavanagh's 'Lough Derg', detailing a visit in 1941

elements.name: Rights
texts: Citation for the purposes of criticism

elements.name: Text
texts:
“On Lough Derg, too, the silver strands
Of the individual sometimes show
Through the fabric of prison anonymity.
One man’s private trouble transcending the divinity
Of the prayer-locked multitude.
A vein of humanity that can bleed
Through the thickest hide.”
